Career Stats & Summary - Rugby League Project|website=Rugbyleagueproject.org}} |updated = 17 December 2020 | module = {{infobox person | embed = yes | father = | mother = | spouse = | education = | alma_mater = | relatives = [[Joel Tomkins]] (brother)
[[Sam Tomkins]] (brother) }} }} '''Logan Tomkins''' is a retired [[rugby league]] footballer who most recently played as a {{rlp|hk}} for the [[Widnes Vikings]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. He has played for the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]], and on loan from [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at the [[Widnes Vikings]] and [[Workington Town]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], and the [[Salford Red Devils]] in the top flight. Tomkins has also spent four seasons at [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] after his loan spell there. ==Background== Tomkins was born in [[Wigan]], [[Greater Manchester]], England. He is the younger brother of fellow professionals [[Joel Tomkins|Joel]] and [[Sam Tomkins]]. ==Playing career== ===Wigan=== Tomkins made his début in Super League on 11 March 2012 in Wigan's away match at his former [[Rugby league dual registration|dual registration]] club; Widnes.{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/17264196|title=Widnes Vikings 37-36 Wigan Warriors|work=BBC Sport|date= 11 March 2012|access-date=9 April 2013}} He played in the [[2013 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over [[Hull F.C.]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].{{cite web|url= https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/23794947|title= Hull FC 0-16 Wigan Warriors|date=24 August 2013|work= BBC Sport|access-date=13 November 2019}}{{cite news|url= https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2013/aug/24/hull-fc-wigan-warriors-challenge-cup-final|title= Wigan Warriors overcome Hull FC and elements to win Challenge Cup|date=24 August 2013|newspaper=[[The Guardian]]|access-date=13 November 2019}}{{cite web|url= http://www.skysports.com/rugbyleague/match_report/0,,11070_3660839,00.html|title= Wigan Warriors grind out victory over Hull|date=24 August 2013|work= Sky Sports|access-date=13 November 2019}} ====Widnes==== During 2011, Tomkins played 14 times on dual registration at [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]]. He scored 6 tries for the then [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]] Side. ====Salford loans==== In 2014, Logan joined [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] on a loan deal. He made his first appearance for the club against [[Bradford Bulls]] in Salford's 38-24 win. In his third appearance, against [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]], Logan scored his team's only try in Salford's 32-4 defeat. On 22 April 2014, Logan agreed to stay with Salford for the rest of the 2014 season.{{Cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12207/9279322/super-league-logan-tomkins-extends-loan-spell-with-salford-red-devils|title=Super League: Logan Tomkins extends loan spell with Salford Red Devils|website=Sky Sports}} Tomkins returned to Salford on loan again in 2015. ====Workington==== Also in 2015, Tomkins played two games for [[Workington Town]] on dual registration from Wigan. ===Salford=== Logan Tomkins made the permanent switch to Salford on 25 November 2015, signing a one-year deal for the [[Super League XXI|2016 season]].{{Cite web |url=http://www.loverugbyleague.com/news_20213-logan-tomkins-commits-to-salford.html |title=Logan Tomkins commits to Salford |access-date=25 November 2015 |archive-date=25 November 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20151125233121/http://www.loverugbyleague.com/news_20213-logan-tomkins-commits-to-salford.html |url-status=dead }} He played in the [[2019 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] at [[Old Trafford]].{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2019/10/12/st-helens-vs-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live/|title=St Helens give Justin Holbrook the perfect send-off with commanding Grand Final victory over Salford|publisher=Telegraph|date=12 October 2019|access-date=25 October 2019}}{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2019/oct/12/st-helens-v-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live/|title=St Helens 23-6 Salford Red Devils: Super League Grand Final – as it happened|newspaper=[[The Guardian]]}}{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/11777123/st-helens-win-2019-super-league-league-leaders-shield/|title=St Helens win 2019 Super League League Leaders' shield|website=Sky Sports}} ===Widnes=== Tomkins joined Widnes Vikings ahead of the [[2020 RFL Championship]], on a one-year deal.{{cite news | url=https://widnesvikings.co.uk/logan-tomkins-joins-the-vikings/ | title=Logan Tomkins joins the Vikings | access-date=25 January 2025 | publisher=Widnes Vikings RLFC}} After signing a one-year extension, he announced his retirement at the end of the [[2021 RFL Championship|2021 season]].{{cite web | url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/widnes-logan-tomkins-retirement | title=Widnes announce Logan Tomkins retirement | publisher=Love Rugby League | date=16 December 2021 | access-date=25 January 2025 | first=Steven | last=Hughes}} ==Family== Logan Tomkins is the youngest brother of former [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] teammates [[Joel Tomkins]] and [[Sam Tomkins]].{{Cite news|url=http://www.theguardian.com/sport/2017/mar/11/logan-tomkins-escapes-shadow-brothers-salford|title=Logan Tomkins escapes shadow of elder brothers in new life at Salford|date=11 March 2017|newspaper=[[The Guardian]]}} ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links==
